title: GT Maru Typeface
description: Very cute website introducing GT Maru font's background and history through animated illustration triggered on scroll.
credits: Grilli Type, Josh Schaub, Sensor Station

analysis.title: GT Maru exclusively at Grilli Type — Download Free Trial Fonts

        GT
THICK SUSHI ROLL with colorful fillings
Sometimes also called Maki Sushi The diameter is about 5–6cm With lotus root, unagi, and anago.
JP Omelette Rice Topped with Ketchup
Originated around the turn of the 20th century @ a restaurant called Renga-tei in Ginza, Tokyo.
From Osaka ©1935,where a street vendor named Tomekichi Endo is credited with its invention.
Originally derives from Hamburg in Germany. Comes with lettuce, bacon, cucumbers, and cheese.
EMPTY THE GLASS! Usual Japanese toast
Important part of Nominication: A drinking party phenomenon most often held in an izakaya.
The original plant was discovered near the Jōdo Temple in Inno-shima.

During one of his trips to Japan, Thierry couldn’t help but admire the casual and friendly tone of wayfinding systems fo
In Switzerland, construction signage is designed with stark geometric shapes. The red, black, and white signs focus on w
Japanese construction signage sometimes takes on a personality of its own. Plain metal poles are replaced with joyful an
This simple observation sparked an interest in designing a typeface that could communicate in a casual yet functional ma
Many signs in Japan are still painted by brush. The letters often showcase rounded terminals and a natural flow that is
In Switzerland, it’s rare to spot hand-painted signs. Instead, most signs are printed in austere sans serifs.

Design
    
  


  
    
      
        Over the course of the next four years, Thierry carefully drafted countless iter
Over the course of the next four years, Thierry carefully drafted countless iterations of rounded letters, paying homage
The Japanese writing system is based on each character taking up one square block, while Latin letters usually vary in w
Finding harmony between the human hand and machine is the essence of GT Maru. The monolinear strokes are softened by the
For a more casual look, the typeface also offers alternative single-story versions of a and g.

Mono
    
  


  
    
      
        GT Maru Mono celebrates the square character frame used to compose Japanese charac
GT Maru Mono celebrates the square character frame used to compose Japanese characters by fully embracing the aesthetic
Letters & Characters have the same amount of horizontal space.
They are widely used in early computers and computer terminals. They often had limited graphical capabilities because of
A typewriter is a machine for typing characters similar to those produced by a printer’s movable type. Usually a typewri
The first commercial typewriters were introduced in 1874, but did not become common in offices until after the mid-1880s

Mega
    
  

  
    
      
        
      
    
  

  
    
      
        Breaking free from the constraints of body
Breaking free from the constraints of body text, GT Maru Mega takes the liberty to pump the design language up a notch.
The Mega subfamily also comes with outlined, shaded, and glowing variants. A throwback to the glorious age of 70s rubdow

Emoji
    
  

  
    
      
        The worldwide phenomenon of emoji traces its creation back to Japan. Taking cues f
The worldwide phenomenon of emoji traces its creation back to Japan. Taking cues from their birthplace, GT Maru features

Overview
    
  

  
    
      
        The GT Maru family features five weights in both proportional and monospaced wi
The GT Maru family features five weights in both proportional and monospaced with matching oblique styles. The expansive
Typeface design by Thierry Blancpain 2017–2021, exclusively available at Grilli Type. Emojis by Grilli Type with Anya Da

worldSystems.name: Playable Poster
worldSystems.reason: tags include typography; tags include colorful; tags include animation

worldSystems.name: Collage Core
worldSystems.reason: tags include colorful; archetype affinity: playable poster

archetype.name: Playable Poster
archetype.reason: tags include colorful; tags include typography; tags include animation; description mentions typeface

mechanics.interactionModel: One-screen poster composition with one or two high-conviction interactions such as drag, reveal, type morph, or hover distort.
mechanics.navigationModel: The page behaves like one campaign object or a short sequence of staged frames.
mechanics.stateModel: Poster at rest -> big reveal -> one interactive transformation -> rest or reset.
engineHints: Use motion/react or GSAP for bold but finite timelines.
Use SVG path animation and variable font APIs for draw-on or type-shift moments.
Keep the DOM shallow; the composition should feel authored, not modular.
motionRecipe: Use oversized type or graphic elements as moving actors, not background garnish.
Keep only one hero gesture alive on mobile.
mechanics.mobileFallback: Split the poster into two to four scenes, preserve one hero interaction, and drop hover-only secondary behavior.
implementationNotes: Use scale, typography, and dead space aggressively; do not fill every region with content.
Keep the interaction count low and the conviction high.
If a visual move is the identity, it should land before the user sees the CTA.
The capture suggests anchored framing or sticky context; preserve that orientation device in the rebuild.
antiPatterns: Do not let the page collapse into a static poster with a button pasted on it.
Do not attach random microinteractions to every element.
Do not keep a desktop-only hover trick as the main story.
validationChecklist: The hero gesture is obvious in under three seconds.
Desktop and mobile preserve the same campaign idea with different staging.
Typography remains part of the interaction system.

designGuidance.implementationPrompt: Build this as a Playable Poster page with a secondary cue from Collage Core using the Playable Poster interaction model. Keep GT Maru as the voice anchor, preserve the palette around #0067fe, #fcdede, #f4f453, drive the page through scroll, tap, hover, type, and implement the mobile fallback as: Split the poster into two to four scenes, preserve one hero interaction, and drop hover-only secondary behavior.
designGuidance.summary: Playable Poster + Playable Poster. One-screen poster composition with one or two high-conviction interactions such as drag, reveal, type morph, or hover distort.
